title: DeepWiki Scale: Depth Parity
weight_rationale: A focused, single-subsystem change in the Phase 4 grounded writing path: one new deterministic module (depth budget), an additive optional gate inside the existing claim-plan validator, prompt/metric hardening, and tests. It is opt-in (expanded grounded mode only), off by default at the command level, and reversible. It earns more than a mini spec because it intentionally changes the pass/fail outcome of an existing expanded path and must preserve strict grounding, benchmark quarantine, and validator strength; it does not earn full weight because there is no new service, data store, interface, or cross-team dependency.
phase_relation: Next phase after deepwiki-scale-core-fanout (commit 35d5d6f). Builds on the breadth anti-compression work; does not replace it.

targets.artifact_being_designed: A source-grounded GeneratedSection depth/detail-density quality contract for the Phase 4 grounded claim plan.
targets.depth_metric: Source-derived per-section detail budget: per required topic, claims must reflect the Phase 3 mapped-evidence density (one grounded claim per mapped evidence id, bounded by an injectable cap); a fanned-out but shallow plan fails before assembly.
non_targets: copying benchmark page counts, headings, prose, or structure
reintroducing deepwiki-scale as a separate product (it stays a behavior-identical alias)

comparator_isolation: ragflow-deepwiki.md is comparison-only: never evidence, prompt seed, headings, prose, structure, or generated required topics. Used only to explain the scale gap and as a post-generation dashboard.
validator_writer_separation: Single process; writer and validator/approver roles are explicitly simulated as separate internal steps in Phase 6 and recorded in working/validation-report.json + working/repair-log.md.
